markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Sighting Shot](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2057)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Light" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=100" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 82

**Price** 72 gp

<row gap="medium">
**Ammunition** any

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Two Actions" /><row>([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Eyes are carved onto a _sighting shot_. When you Activate the ammunition, it glows, shedding [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=407) for 20 feet and [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=408) for 20 feet beyond that. If you shot the activated ammunition, your mind swirls with images of what the _sighting shot_ passed and hit as if you sprinted along the ammunition's course. You see this path as if with your normal visual senses. Once a _sighting shot_ hits anything or reaches its maximum range, it stops transmitting images to you. A _sighting shot's_ light is visible to creatures who didn't Activate the ammunition, but they receive no special information from it.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Sighting Shot

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 19"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Spell Echo Shot](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2058)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 83

**Price** 5,500 gp

<row gap="medium">
**Ammunition** any

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Generous amounts of [djezet](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1410) and [orichalcum](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2919) mix in a _spell echo shot_. When you Activate it, you name up to four creatures, in addition to you, that the ammunition's magic works for. When _spell echo shot_ strikes a target, which can be a square, it remains intact. It moves with a creature it struck, unless the GM determines otherwise, until that creature regains any Hit Points. If it doesn't stick to the target, the active ammunition instead falls into the target's space, remaining active. If you or one of the four selected creatures include the ammunition in the area of a spell that is 5th rank or lower; has an area of a burst, cone, or line; and does not have a duration, the materials in the ammunition immediately duplicate that spell with the same parameters. A duplicated spell can't be duplicated again by another effect. Duplicating a spell destroys the ammunition, which otherwise remains active for 1 minute.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Spell Echo Shot

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Weapon Shot](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2059)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 83

<row gap="medium">
**Ammunition** any

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The body of a _weapon shot_ is translucent and filled with quicksilver. It imparts its magic to the weapon used to fire it or it summons a translucent weapon, made of force, to fire it. It's a favorite of killers and sharpshooters who need just one shot in a situation where carrying ammunition is easier than carrying a weapon.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561))    **Effect** For the Strike with which you consume the ammunition, use the ammunition's fundamental runes, according to its type, instead of those of the weapon firing it.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))    **Effect** A ghostly weapon made of force appears, wielded by you and loaded with the _weapon shot_ you activated. The conjured weapon sublimates into motes of briefly luminous dust if the _weapon shot_ deactivates without you using it or just after you use the activated shot. For the Strike with which it functions, the weapon uses the ammunition's fundamental runes, according to its type.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 84 **Price** 250 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Activate**([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) --- A _captivating score_ is a piece of parchment prepared for musical notation, long enough for a short song. You must be trained in [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) to properly compose this missive (or trained in an appropriate [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) skill your GM allows). You choose one of the emotions described below and write a musical composition to convey that emotion about a subject of your choice, typically a person or group. When activated, the missive plays a note-perfect rendition of the inscribed score. Each creature that can hear the score must succeed at a DC 28 Will save or be influenced to feel the emotion you chose toward the subject of your composition. This lasts for 1 hour, typically returning the creature to its initial attitude unless new events have altered their attitude long-term.
  • **Positive** Your song engenders sympathy, adoration, joy, or a similar emotion. An affected creature's attitude toward the subject improves by one step, such as from [indifferent](/Conditions.aspx?ID=25) to [friendly](/Conditions.aspx?ID=18).
  • **Negative** Your song spurs anger, sadness, jealousy, or a similar emotion. An affected creature's attitude toward the subject worsens by one step, such as from indifferent to [unfriendly](/Conditions.aspx?ID=40).
When composing this missive, you can add a small notation or illustration of a type of instrument. If you do, the music sounds like that instrument, and if you didn't specify, it sounds vaguely like a recorder. The choice of a louder instrument doesn't make the music carry farther.
name: Captivating Score

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Explosive Missive](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2061)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Missive" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=485" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 84

**Price** 13 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Two Actions" /><row>([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

An _explosive missive_ is slightly warm to the touch, regardless of the surrounding environment. When composed and then activated, the missive explodes. The tenor of what you write or the theme of your illustration determines the damage type and adds the corresponding trait to the missive: acid for a caustic inscription, cold for an aloof one, electricity for an energetic one, fire for an angry one, or sonic for an overly emphatic one. The missive deals 4d6 damage to each creature in a 5-foot burst from a corner of the missive's space (DC 18 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). A creature who rolls a critical failure also takes 1d4 [persistent damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) of the same type. The missive burns to ash while releasing its magic.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Explosive Missive

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Glimmering Missive](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2062)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Light" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=100" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Missive" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=485" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 85

**Price** 20 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Two Actions" /><row>([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A _glimmering missive_ sparkles as you compose it. When activated, it explodes, disintegrating into a shower of multicolored motes in a 10-foot burst from a corner of the missive's space. Creatures in the area are covered in sparkling dust that remains luminous for 1 hour. Visible creatures can't be concealed while covered by the luminous dust; any [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=26) creatures are [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) while covered in the luminous dust, rather than being [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=39).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Glimmering Missive

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Heartening Missive](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2063)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Missive" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=485" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 85

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Two Actions" /><row>([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

You compose a _heartening missive_ by creating a short message or image intended to grant the recipient moral support. You must dedicate the missive to an individual creature you know and address it to their location (typically the settlement where you think they are). Once you finish composing the missive, it folds itself into the shape of an animal and [Flies](/Actions.aspx?ID=94) at a speed of 45 feet (about 15 miles per hour) toward the location for up to 24 hours. It alights near the recipient or in their hand. After Activating the missive, the recipient gets its benefit and becomes temporarily immune to all _heartening missives_ for 24 hours.

 If the missive fails to reach its recipient in 24 hours, it returns to its sender at the same pace, becoming non-magical when it arrives. After imparting or losing its magic, the missive remains as a normal document.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 6"
pfs="Standard"
>
Heartening Missive (Bull)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 85

**Price** 50 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

Folded into a bull, this missive grants the recipient a sense of solidity. The recipient gains a +1 item bonus to Fortitude saves for the next 24 hours.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 5"
pfs="Standard"
>
Heartening Missive (Butterfly)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 85

**Price** 25 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

Folded into a delicate butterfly, this missive grants the recipient a sense of grace and poise. Once during the next 24 hours, when the recipient fails a [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) or [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) check, they can improve the result from a failure to a success or from a critical failure to a failure. This is a [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=76) effect.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 6"
pfs="Standard"
>
Heartening Missive (Rabbit)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 85

**Price** 50 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

Folded into a rabbit sitting on its haunches, this missive grants the recipient a sense of mischief and agility. The recipient gains a +1 item bonus to Reflex saves for the next 24 hours.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 6"
pfs="Standard"
>
Heartening Missive (Turtle)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 85

**Price** 50 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

Folded into a square-shelled turtle, this missive grants the recipient a firm sense of self and serenity. The recipient gains a +1 item bonus to Will saves for the next 24 hours.
name: Heartening Missive

>
[Heartening Missive](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2063)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Missive" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=485" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 85

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Two Actions" /><row>([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

You compose a _heartening missive_ by creating a short message or image intended to grant the recipient moral support. You must dedicate the missive to an individual creature you know and address it to their location (typically the settlement where you think they are). Once you finish composing the missive, it folds itself into the shape of an animal and [Flies](/Actions.aspx?ID=94) at a speed of 45 feet (about 15 miles per hour) toward the location for up to 24 hours. It alights near the recipient or in their hand. After Activating the missive, the recipient gets its benefit and becomes temporarily immune to all _heartening missives_ for 24 hours.

 If the missive fails to reach its recipient in 24 hours, it returns to its sender at the same pace, becoming non-magical when it arrives. After imparting or losing its magic, the missive remains as a normal document.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

Folded into a bull, this missive grants the recipient a sense of solidity. The recipient gains a +1 item bonus to Fortitude saves for the next 24 hours.

</traits>

**Price** 25 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

Folded into a delicate butterfly, this missive grants the recipient a sense of grace and poise. Once during the next 24 hours, when the recipient fails a [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) or [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) check, they can improve the result from a failure to a success or from a critical failure to a failure. This is a [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=76) effect.

Folded into a rabbit sitting on its haunches, this missive grants the recipient a sense of mischief and agility. The recipient gains a +1 item bonus to Reflex saves for the next 24 hours.

</row>

</column>

---

A _messenger missive_ sends itself. When composing the missive, you write a location upon it. You can also include an individual creature you expect to be in that location as a recipient; if you don't, the first creature in the location to touch the missive is treated as the recipient. Once you finish composing the missive, it folds itself into the shape of a bird and [Flies](/Actions.aspx?ID=94) at a Speed of 45 feet (15 miles per hour) toward the location for up to 24 hours. It alights near its recipient or in their hand. When activated, the missive becomes non-magical but retains its contents. If it fails to reach its recipient in 24 hours, the missive burns to ash.

</column>

**Price** 60 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

You can write up to four locations on the missive, one on each edge of the paper; you can include a recipient for each location, as well. When you finish composing the missive, it folds into four identical missive birds, each one flying to one of the locations carrying the same message.
name: Messenger Missive

Composed to look like an important document, a _red-handed missive_ is a trap used by those who suspect someone has been going through their correspondence.     right="Item 5"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Stage Fright Missive](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2066)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Curse" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=38" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=106" />
<trait label="Missive" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=485" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 85

**Price** 22 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Two Actions" /><row>([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Composing a _stage fright missive_ usually involves creating a scathing review, insulting letter, or embarrassing image that ridicules the recipient. The activating creature must succeed at a DC 20 Will save or be overcome with embarrassment for 1 hour, taking a –1 status penalty to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5), [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6), [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7), and [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) checks. During this time, if the creature attempts to speak or perform in front of an audience, they become [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34). When they recover from this sickened condition, the missive's effects end. You choose when composing the missive whether it remains as a non-magical document or burns to ash after imparting its magic.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Stage Fright Missive

_Bane oil_ comes in as many forms as the [_bane_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1041) weapon property rune. Coating a weapon with the oil gives the weapon the benefit of one type of _bane_ rune for 1 minute. <title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Fundamental Oil](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2070)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Oil" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=121" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 86

**Price** 220 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Made of elemental salts and essences from the [Inner Sphere](/Rules.aspx?ID=307) or where such planes leak onto [the Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=26), _fundamental oil_ is anathema to elementals and other creatures with a weakness to elemental damage. A weapon anointed with this oil acts as [_bane oil_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2068) to elementals, but the damage type is the same as the target’s greatest weakness if the target has [weakness](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) to acid, cold, electricity, fire, or sonic damage. If it has none of these, the additional damage is the same type as the weapon’s damage type. These effects last 1 minute.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Fundamental Oil

<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

When you apply sticky, stinging _life-boosting oil_, you gain [fast healing](/Rules.aspx?ID=796) according to the oil's type that starts the first time you take damage while the oil lasts. Once the fast healing starts, the oil remains effective for 4 rounds. However, the oil lasts only 8 hours, whether it provides fast healing or not.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Oil of Skating](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2074)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Oil" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=121" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 87

**Price** 5 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This thin, slippery oil shimmers with a golden hue. Coating your shoes or feet with _oil of skating_ enables you to skate quickly along flat surfaces for 1 hour. You gain a +10-foot status bonus to your Speed, which doubles if you move on a downhill surface. You lose this bonus if moving on difficult terrain, greater difficult terrain, or [uneven ground](/Rules.aspx?ID=456). Also, you treat any uphill movement as moving on difficult terrain while your feet are oiled and treat the results of any [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) checks made to [Balance](/Actions.aspx?ID=28) as one degree worse.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Oil of Skating

    pfs="Standard"
>
[Serpent Oil](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2076)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Oil" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=121" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 87

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This glistening oil has a green hue and tiny snake scales floating within. If you slather _serpent oil_ on a Tiny object that is snakelike in shape, from a stick to a scarf, the object transforms into a [viper](/Monsters.aspx?ID=379), keeping some of the same colors and patterns of the original item. If placed on other objects, the oil fails and is wasted. This false snake has the [minion](/Traits.aspx?ID=109) trait. It remains in snake form for 1 minute before returning to its object state. If slain, the item returns to its original form, unharmed.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Serpent Oil](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2076)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Oil" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=121" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 87

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This glistening oil has a green hue and tiny snake scales floating within. If you slather _serpent oil_ on a Tiny object that is snakelike in shape, from a stick to a scarf, the object transforms into a [viper](/Monsters.aspx?ID=379), keeping some of the same colors and patterns of the original item. If placed on other objects, the oil fails and is wasted. This false snake has the [minion](/Traits.aspx?ID=109) trait. It remains in snake form for 1 minute before returning to its object state. If slain, the item returns to its original form, unharmed.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 7"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Weapon-Weird Oil](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2077)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Oil" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=121" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 87

**Price** 55 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Each dose of _weapon-weird oil_ is keyed to a particular melee weapon group, selected from among [axe](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=1), [brawling](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=4), [club](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=5), [flail](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=7), [hammer](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=8), [knife](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=9), [pick](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=10), [polearm](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=11), [shield](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=12), [spear](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=14), and [sword](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=15). The oil creates a synergy between skill and weapon, enabling you to wield the weapon in unexpected ways. You must have proficiency with the original weapon to benefit from the oil; however, you use your proficiency rank with the oil's keyed group instead of the weapon's original group. Also, you apply the [critical specialization effect](/Rules.aspx?ID=235) from the oil's keyed group instead of the weapon's normal critical specialization effect. While the oil remains effective, the [_grievous_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=298) rune and similar magic react as if the weapon belongs to the oil's group. A weapon can be coated in only one type of _weapon-weird oil_ at a time. Any new application of this oil supersedes any previous one. These effects last for 1 hour.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Weapon-Weird Oil

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 88 **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L **Activate**([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) --- _Cayden's brew_ is like rich beer or ale, with a golden-brown color and foamy head. For 1 hour after you drink it, you have a +1 item bonus to saving throws against [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68) effects. Also, you can use a single action to breathe out a 15-foot cone of intoxicating vapor with a burp that can be heard for 100 feet. Any creature in the vapor must attempt a DC 25 Fortitude saving throw. After you unleash this magical burp, you can’t do so again for 1d4 rounds. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [clumsy 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3) and [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) for 1 round.
**Failure** The creature is clumsy 1 and stupefied 1 for 1d4 rounds.
**Critical Failure** The creature becomes clumsy 2, stupefied 2, and [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34). The clumsy and stupefied conditions last until 1d4 rounds after the sickened condition ends. Cayden's Brew **Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 88 **Price** 80 gp **Bulk** L Cayden's Brew (Double) **Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 88 **Price** 480 gp **Bulk** L --- The save DC is 31. Cayden's Brew (Triple) **Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 88 **Price** 3,200 gp **Bulk** L --- The save DC is 40.
name: Cayden's Brew

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 18"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Cloning Potion](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2080)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Potion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=130" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 88

**Price** 5,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

When you drink a _cloning potion_, you split in two, one version of you remaining in your space and the other moving into an adjacent space. Your clone, which has the [minion](/Traits.aspx?ID=109) trait, looks like you and remains for 1 minute. Provided you are both on the same plane, you can command your clone telepathically with a single action with the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) trait. You can also issue verbal commands, as normal for a minion. As an action that has the concentrate trait, you can sense through your clone. When you do, you lose all sensory information from your own body. You can Dismiss this sense-sharing effect.

 You and your clone share Hit Points, and the clone uses your statistics. Spells and effects target you and your clone as if you are separate creatures. The clone can Activate your abilities and Cast your Spells, limited by its number of actions, but you share elements such as use limits, frequency, and spell slots. In other words, your clone using one of your resources works as if you did so in or from your clone's space. Your clone has gear identical to your own, excepting [artifacts](/Traits.aspx?ID=253) and [consumables](/Traits.aspx?ID=36). Any item taken from the clone melts into silvery dust within seconds, with ammunition or thrown objects lasting just long enough to travel to and possibly hit their target.

 When the duration lapses, your clone melts into silvery dust. You become temporarily immune to _cloning potion_ for 1d4 days.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Cloning Potion

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Eidetic Potion](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2081)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Potion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=130" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 89

**Price** 40 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Bright pink, pale lavender, and vibrant orange colors swirl through this purplish liquid, making the _eidetic potion_ resemble an unforgettable sunset. When you drink this potion, for 1 round, anything you observe becomes locked into your memory. You can recall the memory perfectly and gain a +2 status bonus to create representations of that memory, whether using [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) to create an artistic rendition or [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) to create a [Forgery](/Actions.aspx?ID=60). The memory remains locked for 1 week or until you benefit from the bonus it imparts for the first time, whichever comes first; afterward, it becomes a normal, fallible memory. You can only have a single memory locked in your mind at a time. Using this potion while you have a locked memory from a previous use makes the previously locked memory fallible but allows you to lock a new memory.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Eidetic Potion

    level="1"
    right="Item 12"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Elysian Dew](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2082)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Potion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=130" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 89

**Price** 375 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

When you drink sweet, sky-blue _Elysian dew_, for 1 minute, you gain a 10-foot aura that evokes the vitality of [Elysium](/Planes.aspx?ID=16), causing nearby objects to seem more colorful and plants to stand taller. You and any ally that starts its turn in the emanation gain 5 temporary Hit Points, a +1 item bonus to saving throws, and a +1 item bonus to [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) and [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) checks until the start of your or the ally's next turn. If you're evil and drink this potion, it fails to work, and you must succeed at a DC 30 Fortitude save or the potion renders you [drained 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Elysian Dew

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The pale liquid of _empath's cordial_ changes color to reflect the mood of the creature nearest to it—turning blue for calm, red for anger, and green for envious, among other hues. For 1 hour after you drink this potion, you can sense the presence of general emotions, such as hostility toward you or the presence of an [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60) effect impacting a creature's emotions, within 30 feet. A creature that failed a saving throw against [_calm emotions_](/Spells.aspx?ID=31) can't be detected. This potion doesn't allow you to automatically tell what emotions a specific creature is experiencing, but you can attempt a Perception check with a +2 item bonus (DC set by the GM) to discern that information. The potion also grants you a +1 item bonus to Perception checks to [Sense Motive](/Actions.aspx?ID=85) against creatures whose emotions you can sense.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Empath's Cordial

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Enigma-Sight Potion](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2084)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Potion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=130" />
<trait label="Revelation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=138" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 89

**Price** 250 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A vial of this _enigma-sight_ potion seems to contain fragments of kaleidoscopic crystal that reflect mind-bending scenes. However, these “crystals” act like liquid when poured or consumed. Drinking the potion grants you the effects of a [_truesight_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727) spell, except that you use your [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2298) modifier and half your level, rounded up, for the [secret](/Traits.aspx?ID=690) [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) check instead of the normal counteract modifier and spell rank. However, if you critically fail the secret counteract check the effect grants, your mind fills in false, nightmarish information. You become [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 for 1d4 rounds and stunned 1 as your brain struggles to process what you see.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Enigma-Sight Potion

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 7"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Hovering Potion](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2086)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Potion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=130" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 90

**Price** 55 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A foamy, cloudy liquid, _hovering potion_ enables you to defy gravity for 5 minutes. By default, the potion causes you to float several inches off the ground. If you take a Stride action, you can move 10 feet up or down in the air. You can instead [Climb](/Actions.aspx?ID=33) at your full Speed or Stride to move along a horizontal surface at half your Speed. The GM determines which surfaces can be climbed or moved across this way. If you fall while this potion is in effect, you can use the [Arrest a Fall](/Actions.aspx?ID=91) reaction as if you had a fly Speed. If you have a fly Speed, you remain airborne at the end of your turn, even if you didn't use a [Fly](/Actions.aspx?ID=94) action this round.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Hovering Potion

</title>

<traits>

---

When you drink the thick, slate-colored _immovable potion_, you become anchored in place, even defying gravity, rendering you [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24) for 1 minute or until you Dismiss the activation. While you are immobilized this way, the DC to move you from your place, including knocking you [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31), is 40.

</column>

</title>

<traits>

<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=106" />
<trait label="Possession" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=129" />
<trait label="Potion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=130" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 90

**Price** 1,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Small bolts of brightly colored electricity flicker through the cloudy _mind-swap potion_. The potion often comes in a double-chambered flask, because when you drink it, you consume half the contents. If another creature of the same ancestry consumes the remainder of the contents within 1 minute, your minds swap per the effect of a critical success on a [_mind swap_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=61) ritual. The effects last for 24 hours or until one of you Dismisses the activation.

_Quenching potion_ is a clear liquid that tastes like fresh, cool spring water. Drinking this potion or pouring it over yourself completely hydrates you and cleanses your system. Immediately attempt a [flat check](/Rules.aspx?ID=333) to end any [persistent acid, fire, poison, or void damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) affecting you, and attempt a new saving throw against any [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669) affecting you. Poison can’t progress to a worse stage due to this saving throw. The potion counts as a particularly appropriate type of help against persistent acid, fire, poison, and void damage.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Quenching Potion

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 9"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Rebirth Potion](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2090)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Potion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=130" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 90

**Price** 150 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A small bit from a [humanoid](/Traits.aspx?ID=91)—such as a hair, scale, or feather—steeps in the clear liquid of a _rebirth potion_. When the potion is created, this bit determines the ancestry and heritage the potion changes the imbiber to. After you drink the potion, you transform into that ancestry over 8 hours during your next period of rest, finishing the transformation after the 8 hours are up. Throughout this time, you are [clumsy 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3), [enfeebled 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13), and [stupefied 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37). Once the transformation is complete, the potion's magic ends and can't be [counteracted](/Rules.aspx?ID=371). Replace your ancestry Hit Points, size, Speeds, ability boosts, ability flaws, traits, and special abilities with those of your new ancestry. You lose your ancestry feats, selecting replacements valid for your new ancestry. You have mild control over the change, but you end up with a unique appearance fitting for your new ancestry, and some quirks of your body remain, such as relative age, general health, scars, and missing digits, limbs, or organs.

 Drinking a _rebirth potion_ of your current ancestry works normally, allowing you to rearrange some of the cited ancestry elements and change your appearance (provided you abide by the potion's limitations regarding health and age).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Rebirth Potion

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 13+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Rovagug's Mud](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2092)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Potion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=130" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 90

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

_Rovagug's mud_ smells and tastes like wet, sour earth. For 1 hour after you drink it, you have a +2 item bonus to saving throws against [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93) effects. Also, you can use a single action to breathe out a 20-foot cone of bitter vapor that causes an earth-shaking rumble that can be heard for 100 feet. Creatures and objects in the area take 5d6 damage (DC 30 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save), decreasing Hardness by 5. An object's Hardness remains lowered for 1d4 rounds, and you can't use this breath again for the same amount of time.

</column>

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 20"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Starsong Nectar](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2093)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Fortune" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=76" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Potion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=130" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 91

**Price** 14,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A liquid with the appearance of an expressive night sky and a taste that’s sheer pleasure, _starsong nectar_ grants you cheerful confidence and incredible luck for 10 minutes after you drink it. However, if you show too much caution at any point during this time (GM’s discretion), you must succeed at a DC 6 [flat check](/Rules.aspx?ID=333) or the potion’s effects end. While the potion lasts, you gain a +3 status bonus to attack rolls, Perception checks, saving throws, and skill checks, and you aren’t [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) to creatures due to them [flanking](/Rules.aspx?ID=2375) you or being [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=79) from or undetected by you. You are temporarily immune to this potion for 24 hours once its effects end.

While the potion lasts, though, if you take a moment to imagine the future, you choose the best courses of action. This aspect of the potion grants you the following activation.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per round;   **Effect** Until the start of your next turn, roll two d20s for any attack rolls, Perception checks, saving throws, and skill checks you make and take the higher result.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Starsong Nectar

<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Potion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=130" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 91

**Price** 17 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The light-brown liquid of a _sure-step potion_ helps you find your footing. After drinking it, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) checks to [Balance](/Actions.aspx?ID=2369) for 1 hour. In addition, you can [Step](/Actions.aspx?ID=2304) into [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366), and you aren’t [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) on uneven ground.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Sure-Step Potion

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Tentacle Potion](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2095)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Potion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=130" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 91

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Upon consuming this mottled, foul-tasting liquid, the _tentacle potion_ causes you to extrude a long, flexible limb of ectoplasm. Your clothing doesn't need to accommodate this limb of ghostly matter, which can extrude through your clothing and armor. The limb lasts 1 hour, and you can Dismiss the activation. You can't hide or disguise the tentacle. You can use the limb to perform simple Interact actions, such as opening an unlocked door. Your limb can't perform actions that require significant manual dexterity, including any action that would require a check to accomplish. You can't use it to hold items. At one time, you can have only one extra limb from any version of this potion. Stronger tentacle potions replace the effects of weaker ones.

 If you have the [Flexible Tail](/Feats.aspx?ID=2405) or [Skillful Tail](/Feats.aspx?ID=1386) feats, the [Tailed Goblin](/Heritages.aspx?ID=37) heritage, or a similar feature the GM believes would benefit, this potion can instead fortify your tail. A fortified tail benefits from any _tentacle potion_ as if the potion were the next better type.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 6"
pfs="Standard"
>
Tentacle Potion (Lesser)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 91

**Price** 33 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 10"
pfs="Standard"
>
Tentacle Potion (Moderate)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 91

**Price** 155 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

As lesser, but you can use the limb to hold an item of up to light Bulk. You can also use the whole limb to hold onto a suitable anchor point, such as a tree branch, balcony, or rocky outcropping, subject to the GM's discretion. While using your limb this way, you have free use of all your other limbs, so you can perform tasks that require both hands. This potion lasts 8 hours.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 14"
pfs="Standard"
>
Tentacle Potion (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 91

**Price** 660 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

As moderate, but the tail can hold an item of up to 1 Bulk or be used as a second hand to help hold an item of up to 2 Bulk. You can also use the limb to make an [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) attack that deals 1d6 bludgeoning damage and has the [sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=194) trait. This potion lasts 24 hours.
name: Tentacle Potion

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 9"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Amnemonic Charm](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2096)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Talisman" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=155" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 92

**Price** 140 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to armor

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Free Action" /><row>([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561))</row></row>

 

**Trigger** You fail or critically fail a [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5), [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6), or [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) check to [Coerce](/Actions.aspx?ID=52), [Lie](/Actions.aspx?ID=47), [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=50), or [Request](/Actions.aspx?ID=51)

**Requirement** You're a master in the skill you failed with.
</row>

</column>

---

This ornate locket, usually containing a faceless, humanoid portrait, is typically worn on a chain around the neck or attached to your clothing. When activated, the charm casts a DC 26 [_rewrite memory_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1655) spell against a single target of the check, removing all memory of your failed attempt and potentially allowing you to make another attempt. The effect can’t remove more than 3 minutes of memories from its target.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Amnemonic Charm
requirement_markdown: You're a master in the skill you failed with.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Blending Brooch](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2097)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=92" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Talisman" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=155" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 92

**Price** 275 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to armor

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Free Action" /><row>([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561))</row></row>

 

**Trigger** You roll initiative using [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) and can see a creature

**Requirement** You are a master in Stealth.
</row>

</column>

---

This small, matte-black pin always seems to be on the periphery of your vision, even when you stare directly at it. When you Activate the talisman, choose one creature you can see. You become [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=26) to that creature unless it succeeds at a DC 28 Will save. This effect lasts for 1 minute or until the target hits you with an attack, whichever comes first.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Blending Brooch
requirement_markdown: You are a master in Stealth.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 12"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Captivating Bauble](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2098)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Auditory" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=16" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Emotion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=60" />
<trait label="Linguistic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=101" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Talisman" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=155" />
<trait label="Visual" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=163" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 92

**Price** 350 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to armor

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Three Actions" /><row>([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))</row></row>

 

 

**Requirement** You are a master in [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) or [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6).
</row>

</column>

---

This talisman appears as an ornate piece of jewelry of the highest quality. When you Activate it, your speech and mannerisms become supernaturally compelling for up to 1 hour. By engaging an intelligent creature in conversation for at least 1 minute, you can cause them to become [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=14) unless they succeed at a DC 30 Will save. This fascination lasts for as long as you continue conversing or until you move at least 20 feet away. When the effect ends, the target becomes temporarily immune for 24 hours. If you or any ally within 120 feet takes an overtly [hostile action](/Rules.aspx?ID=300) while a creature is fascinated by the bauble, the bauble burns out in a shower of sparks and all its effects end.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Captivating Bauble
requirement_markdown: You are a master in [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) or [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6).

    level="1"
    right="Item 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Chain of Stars](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2099)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Force" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=75" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Talisman" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=155" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 92

**Price** 100 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to a  thrown  weapon

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Free Action" /><row>([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561))</row></row>

 

**Trigger** You hit a creature with the affixed weapon.

 
</row>

</column>

---

This delicate [dawnsilver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2917) chain strung with tiny [shuriken](/Weapons.aspx?ID=439) is wound tightly around the handle of the affixed weapon. When you Activate the chain, three _[+1 striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) shuriken_ made from magical force materialize in the target’s space and split off to attack other creatures. Attempt up to three shuriken Strikes that must each target a different creature and can’t target the creature you hit to trigger this talisman. These Strikes use your attack modifier but originate from the hit creature’s space. These attacks count toward your multiple attack penalty, but the penalty doesn’t increase until after all three attacks have been made. The shuriken deal force damage instead of their normal type, and each shuriken vanishes after its attack.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Chain of Stars

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 16"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Dragonclaw Scutcheon](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2101)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Talisman" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=155" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 92

**Price** 1,600 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to a shield

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Free Action" /><row>([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561))</row></row>

 

**Trigger** You would take damage of a type depending on the talisman’s [dragon](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=395) type.

**Requirement** You have the affixed shield [raised](/Actions.aspx?ID=98).
</row>

</column>

---

This decorative shield emblem contains the gilded claw of a dragon mounted in a setting of high-grade adamantine alloy. It protects against a damage type depending on the type of dragon the claw came from (see sidebar). When you Activate the scutcheon, you and all of your carried, wielded, or worn items gain immunity to all damage of that type until the end of your next turn.

If you have a _dragonclaw scutcheon_, [_dragonscale cameo_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2102), and [_dragontooth trophy_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2103) attached to your items, and they all correspond to the same type of dragon, you gain an extra benefit. When you Activate any of these talismans, you gain the ability to cast an 8th-rank, DC 35 [_dragon breath_](/Spells.aspx?ID=2103) focus spell as an [innate](/Rules.aspx?ID=2232) spell once before the end of your next turn. Use the dragon type that matches the talismans; you can Cast the Spell this way without spending a Focus Point. Because each talisman disintegrates when used, you can’t get this benefit again until you’ve attached a replacement.

**Craft Requirements** Supply one claw from an adult or older [dragon](/Traits.aspx?ID=50).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Dragonclaw Scutcheon

<traits>

<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />

</row>

</column>

---

This imposing fang is engraved with an intricate arrangement of symbols, glyphs, and patterns and dangles from a leather strap bound to the hilt of the affixed weapon. When you Activate the trophy, your weapon is momentarily transformed into a magical construct of draconic fury. On the triggering Strike and until the end of your next turn, the damage type of the affixed weapon changes to the type matching the dragon the claw came from (see the sidebar). This change overrides the [versatile](/Traits.aspx?ID=200) trait and similar abilities.

**Craft Requirements** Supply one tooth from an adult or older [dragon](/Traits.aspx?ID=50).

</column>

[False Death Vial](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2106)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />

<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Free Action" /><row>([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561))</row></row>

 

**Trigger** You would be reduced to 0 Hit Points by damage but not immediately killed

**Requirement** You are unarmored.
</row>

</column>

---

This tiny crystal vial of oily liquid is typically attached to a pin or worn on a tether attached to the affixed article of clothing. When you Activate the vial, you avoid being knocked out and remain at 1 Hit Point, your [wounded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=42) condition increases by 1, and the talisman casts [_drop dead_](/Spells.aspx?ID=92) on you. You and any items you're wearing and holding are instantly transported from your current space to a clear space of your choice within 30 feet. You take no damage from the triggering effect. If you are carrying another creature (even one contained inside an [extradimensional](/Traits.aspx?ID=67) container), it's left behind in your original space.

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 13+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[False Death Vial](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2106)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Talisman" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=155" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 93

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to  explorer's clothing  (or another item in the unarmored defense category)

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Free Action" /><row>([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561))</row></row>

 

**Trigger** You would be reduced to 0 Hit Points by damage but not immediately killed

**Requirement** You are unarmored.
</row>

</column>

---

This tiny crystal vial of oily liquid is typically attached to a pin or worn on a tether attached to the affixed article of clothing. When you Activate the vial, you avoid being knocked out and remain at 1 Hit Point, your [wounded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=42) condition increases by 1, and the talisman casts [_drop dead_](/Spells.aspx?ID=92) on you. You and any items you're wearing and holding are instantly transported from your current space to a clear space of your choice within 30 feet. You take no damage from the triggering effect. If you are carrying another creature (even one contained inside an [extradimensional](/Traits.aspx?ID=67) container), it's left behind in your original space.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 94, [Pathfinder #149: Against the Scarlet Triad](/Sources.aspx?ID=14) pg. 77 **Price** 700 gp **Usage** affixed to a weapon **Activate**([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Trigger** You damage a [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) creature with the affixed weapon. --- This bone statuette of a sneering gremlin crumbles to dust when activated, imparting a fraction of its subject's infamous misfortune on those you strike. The damaged creature must attempt a DC 33 Will save. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature must roll twice and take the worse result on the next check it attempts.
**Failure** The creature must roll twice and take the worse result on all checks until the end of its next turn.
name: Grinning Pugwampi

<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />

<column gap="tiny">

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Free Action" /><row>([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561))</row></row>

 

**Trigger** A creature critically hits you.

 
</row>

</column>

---

This dull black pebble, ominously cold to the touch and curiously heavy for its size, is typically bound to the affixed weapon inside a strip of cloth wrapped around its grip. When you Activate the _grudgestone_, your Strikes with the affixed weapon against the triggering creature gain a +3 status bonus to damage rolls for 1 minute or until the target dies, whichever comes first.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 17"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Hungering Maw](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2109)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Talisman" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=155" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 94

**Price** 2,200 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to armor

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Free Action" /><row>([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561))</row></row>

 

**Trigger** You attempt a saving throw against a [possession](/Traits.aspx?ID=129) effect

**Requirement** You are a master in Will saves.
</row>

</column>

---

A shining onyx gemstone clutched in a pair of black steel jaws, this pendant is a potent defense against hostile spirits and other beings who commandeer the bodies of others. When you Activate it, you gain a +4 item bonus to your saving throw against the triggering effect. If your result prevents you from being possessed, the creature that attempted to possess you is subject to a DC 37 [_bind soul_](/Spells.aspx?ID=21) spell, which can trap it in the talisman as if it had recently died. The talisman's magic is exhausted after use, but a soul trapped this way remains imprisoned inside, as detailed in the spell description. Starting at the end of its next turn, the trapped creature gets a new save to end the effect at the end of each of its turns.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Hungering Maw
requirement_markdown: You are a master in Will saves.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Mortalis Coin](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2110)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Talisman" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=155" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 94

**Price** 20 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to armor

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Free Action" /><row>([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561))</row></row>

 

**Trigger** You are reduced to 0 Hit Points by damage but not immediately killed

**Requirement** You are an expert in Fortitude saves.
</row>

</column>

---

This small golden coin is usually stamped with the image of a boar or other resilient creature. If the triggering damage would cause you to become [dying 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=11) (typically due to a critical hit or your critical failure), you become dying 1 instead. In addition, for 10 minutes, you die from the dying condition at dying 5, rather than dying 4.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Mortalis Coin
requirement_markdown: You are an expert in Fortitude saves.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 13+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Reflecting Shard](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2111)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Talisman" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=155" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 94

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to a shield

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Reaction" /><row>([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561))</row></row>

 

**Trigger** You are targeted by a spell of 5th rank or lower

**Requirement** You're a master in [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3), and you have the affixed shield [raised](/Actions.aspx?ID=98).
</row>

</column>

---

This mirrored metal fragment is bolted or welded to the face of the affixed shield. When you Activate it, you attempt to reflect the triggering spell back at its caster with [_spell riposte_](/Spells.aspx?ID=2027), using your [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) modifier for the [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) check. The talisman’s counteract rank is 7th.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

</title>

level="2"

</row>

</column>

---

The talisman can reflect spells of any level, and its counteract rank is 9th.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Reverberating Stone](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2112)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Talisman" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=155" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 94

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to a shield

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Free Action" /><row>([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561))</row></row>

 

**Trigger** You use the affixed shield to [Shield Block](/Feats.aspx?ID=839) a melee attack

**Requirement** You're a master in [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3), and you have the affixed shield [raised](/Actions.aspx?ID=98).
</row>

</column>

---

This heavy metal emblem is mounted on the face of the affixed shield and has a dull gray gemstone embedded at its center. When you Activate the talisman, the gem shatters, releasing a powerful shockwave in a 15-foot cone that must include the enemy who struck the triggering blow, if possible. Each creature in the cone takes 2d6 sonic damage with a DC 28 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save. Any creature that fails its save is [pushed](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) 5 feet away from you (or 10 feet on a critical failure).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 11"
pfs="Standard"
>
Reverberating Stone
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 94

**Price** 275 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 15"
pfs="Standard"
>
Reverberating Stone (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 94

**Price** 1,200 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The cone is 30 feet, the damage is 3d6, and the DC is 34.
name: Reverberating Stone
requirement_markdown: You're a master in [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3), and you have the affixed shield [raised](/Actions.aspx?ID=98).

**Requirement** You prepared the staff.
</row>

---

This cluster of unworked ruby is bound to the top of a staff by silver wire. When activated, it adds one temporary charge to the staff. Any spells cast from the staff before the end of the turn expend these charges first. Any unused temporary charges are lost at the end of your turn.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 9"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Wooden Nickel](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2115)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=92" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Talisman" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=155" />
<trait label="Visual" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=163" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 95

**Price** 130 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to armor

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Three Actions" /><row>([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561))</row></row>

 

 

**Requirement** You are a master in [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5).
</row>

</column>

---

This rough wooden coin hangs from a string drilled through a hole at its center. When you Activate the _wooden nickel_, for 10 minutes, you can cause any object you touch of 2 Bulk or less to look, feel, and smell like valuables of a similar size crafted from a precious metal of your choice.

 A creature that touches or Interacts with an affected object can attempt a Perception check against your Deception DC to disbelieve the illusion, and a successful check against your Deception DC with [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) or an appropriate [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) skill will also reveal the item's true nature. After 10 minutes have elapsed since you Activated the _wooden nickel_, all affected objects revert to their true form.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Wooden Nickel

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 15"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Bargainer's Instrument](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2116)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 96

**Price** 1,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="" /><row>1 hour ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A _bargainer’s instrument_ is a musical implement. The most typical version is a violin made from maple and spruce, its body stained so deep a purple it appears almost black. When activated, the instrument casts a [_binding circle_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=110) ritual to conjure a willing extraplanar talent to compete with you in performance.

The contest could be a musical battle, a dance-off, an oratorical debate—the two of you could even use entirely different types of performance. The loser of the bout is magically bound to perform a task of the winner’s choice within 1 week. The contest consists of taking turns to [Perform](/Actions.aspx?ID=2401), starting with your opponent. The Performance check’s DC is the higher of the opponent’s [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2298) DC or Will DC, though the two parties can mutually agree to use an impartial judge instead. The first to reach four successes wins, with each critical successes counting as two successes. If you play the bargainer’s instrument as part of your performance, it grants you a +2 item bonus to the Performance checks.

After the contest, the conjured creature can choose to return to where it came from or remain where you conjured it. The loser must endeavor to complete the task by all reasonable means within 1 week. Failing to complete it is shameful but has no further consequences. The instrument loses its magic and remains a non-magical [virtuoso instrument](/Equipment.aspx?ID=37).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Bargainer's Instrument

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 96 **Price** 140 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L **Activate**Strike --- When stared at, a _bewildering spellgun_ seems to warp the space around it, creating a mind-bending sensation. Whispers of gibberish arise from it, making their way to nearby ears despite any other sounds in the area. You Activate the spellgun by aiming it at one creature and making your choice of a spell attack roll or a firearm attack roll against the target's AC. This spellgun has a [range increment](/Traits.aspx?ID=248) of 15 feet. Once fired, the spellgun twists in your hand and melts away. **Critical Success** The target is [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=5) for 1 minute.
**Success** The target is confused for 1 minute but can attempt a DC 25 Will save at the end of each of its turns to end the effect.
name: Bewildering Spellgun

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 96

**Price** 70 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

An _energizing treat_ is a treat made from meat or grains. When you feed your [animal companion](/Rules.aspx?ID=149) or [familiar](/Rules.aspx?ID=160) an energizing treat, it's [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=32) for 1 minute. It can use the extra action each round only for Strike, Stride, and [Support](/Actions.aspx?ID=342) actions, and it can do so only if it normally has those actions available and you take the proper action to command it.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Energizing Treat

>
[Familiar Morsel](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2119)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 96

**Price** 30 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Familiar morsels are little treats that come in a wide variety of flavors that appeal to numerous creatures. Each morsel is keyed to one [familiar ability](Familiars.aspx) at its creation. When you feed the morsel to your familiar, it gains that familiar ability for 1 hour. If your familiar doesn't meet the requirements, or if it already has an ability from a familiar morsel, the morsel is nothing more than a pleasing snack, its magic wasted.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Familiar Morsel

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 97 **Price** 325 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L **Activate**10 minutes ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) --- The bristles on a _miraculous paintbrush_ shift in a rainbow of colors, and can magically create an object painted with them. The paint flows as you Activate the _miraculous paintbrush_ and changes color at your whim as you paint. The paint can cover a 10-foot-square, two-dimensional surface. When you’re done painting, attempt a DC 30 [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) check. **Critical Success** The object you painted emerges from the surface as a real, permanent object no larger than 10 feet in any dimension. It's non-magical and inanimate and has no value except to certain art collectors. If you paint a creature, machine, or form of energy (such as a campfire), these depictions appear only as inanimate sculptures. The created object has a painterly appearance that clearly marks its unusual origin, but is as real as any other.
**Success** As critical success, but the object constantly sheds flakes of paint until it falls apart, destroyed, after 24 hours.
**Failure** The pigments leave a painting that doesn't quite look right and doesn't become an object.
**Critical Failure** Is it a dog? Whatever you made is a pretty lousy painting and doesn't resemble what you set out to make.
name: Miraculous Paintbrush

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 97 **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L **Activate**Strike --- Elegant silver filigree contains the body of this ephemeral item, which is made of solid light. Its shape resembles a pistol, and it’s often carried by hunters of [werecreatures](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=484) and [vampires](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=480). You Activate the spellgun by aiming it at one creature and making your choice of a spell attack roll or a firearm attack roll against the target’s AC. This spellgun has a [range](/Rules.aspx?ID=2195) increment of 30 feet. The spellgun emits a silvery ray of pure moonlight that deals fire damage depending on its type. The spellgun’s damage is treated as [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2920) for the purposes of [weaknesses, resistances, and the like](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312). **Critical Success** The target takes double damage and is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7) until the start of your next turn. If it has a weakness to silver or a resistance that can be bypassed by silver, it's also enfeebled 1 for 1 minute.
**Success** The target takes full damage and is dazzled until the start of your next turn. Moonlit Spellgun (Minor) **Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 97 **Price** 7 gp **Bulk** L --- The damage is 1d8 at night, or 1d6 at other times. Moonlit Spellgun (Lesser) **Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 97 **Price** 30 gp **Bulk** L --- The damage is 3d8 at night, or 3d6 at other times. Moonlit Spellgun (Moderate) **Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 97 **Price** 150 gp **Bulk** L --- The damage is 7d8 at night, or 7d6 at other times. Moonlit Spellgun (Greater) **Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 97 **Price** 600 gp **Bulk** L --- The damage is 12d8 at night, or 12d6 at other times. Moonlit Spellgun (Major) **Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 97 **Price** 3,000 gp **Bulk** L --- The damage is 18d8 at night, or 18d6 at other times.
name: Moonlit Spellgun
