This spellgun has a [range](/Rules.aspx?ID=2195) increment of 30 feet. The spellgun emits a silvery ray of pure moonlight that deals fire damage depending on its type. The spellgun’s damage is treated as [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2920) for the purposes of [weaknesses, resistances, and the like](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312). **Critical Success** The target takes double damage and is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7) until the start of your next turn. If it has a weakness to silver or a resistance that can be bypassed by silver, it's also enfeebled 1 for 1 minute.
**Success** The target takes full damage and is dazzled until the start of your next turn. Moonlit Spellgun (Minor) **Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 97 **Price** 7 gp **Bulk** L --- The damage is 1d8 at night, or 1d6 at other times. Moonlit Spellgun (Lesser) **Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 97 **Price** 30 gp **Bulk** L --- The damage is 3d8 at night, or 3d6 at other times. Moonlit Spellgun (Moderate) **Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 97 **Price** 150 gp **Bulk** L --- The damage is 7d8 at night, or 7d6 at other times. Moonlit Spellgun (Major) **Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 97 **Price** 3,000 gp **Bulk** L --- The damage is 18d8 at night, or 18d6 at other times.

    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Neophyte's Fipple](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2122)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 97

**Price** 8 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561))</row></row>

 

 

**Requirement** You are untrained in [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12).
</row>

</column>

---

Made of polished wood, a _neophyte's fipple_ is a block flute enchanted to guarantee melodic sound. When you Perform a song on the fipple to Activate it, your ability modifier, proficiency bonus, and item bonus for the Performance check total +7, regardless of what they would normally be. Add other bonuses and penalties to the check normally. Once the magic is used, the fipple remains as a mundane [instrument](/Equipment.aspx?ID=37).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Neophyte's Fipple

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Potion Patch](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2123)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 97

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561))</row></row>

 

 

**Requirement** You must have the _potion patch_ affixed to your skin.
</row>

</column>

---

A _potion patch_ is a sticky, bandage-like pad that can be filled with one [potion](/Equipment.aspx?Category=15&Subcategory=18) and affixed to the skin. Filling the patch and affixing it is a 1-minute activity that takes two hands and has the [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104) trait. A patch has a maximum level of potion it can absorb, depending on the patch's type. When you Activate the patch, the potion affects you without you needing to have the potion in your hand. The patch's magic is negated after it's used, the next time you make your daily preparations, or when another _potion patch_ is affixed to you, whichever comes first.

</column>

>

</traits>

**Price** 600 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The potion can be any level.

markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 97 **Price** 600 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L **Activate**Strike --- A rod of blackened bone with a bulb at one end comprises a _reaper's spellgun_, which feels heavier than it looks. You Activate the spellgun by aiming it at one creature and making your choice of a spell attack roll or a firearm attack roll against the target's AC. This spellgun has a [range increment](/Traits.aspx?ID=248) of 30 feet. The spellgun fires a shadowy ray, then dissolves into gray mist. **Critical Success** The target takes 4d6 void damage and is [drained 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10) and [doomed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9). If it had fewer than half its maximum Hit Points after taking the void damage, it's drained 3 instead. If the Hit Point loss from being drained drops the creature to 0 Hit Points, it dies.
**Success** As critical success, but for the drained condition, the target is drained 1, or drained 2 if it had fewer than half its maximum HP.
name: Reaper's Spellgun

markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 98 **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L **Activate**Strike --- A broad wooden tube with a handle, a _sparking spellgun_ radiates warmth. You Activate the spellgun by aiming it at one creature and making your choice of a spell attack roll or a firearm attack roll against the target's AC. This spellgun has a [range increment](/Traits.aspx?ID=248) of 30 feet. The spellgun fires a small ball of sparks and fire, then crumbles to ash. The ball explodes in a flash when it hits, dealing fire damage and [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) according to its type. **Critical Success** The target takes double damage, takes double persistent damage, is [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=1) for 1 round, and is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7) while the persistent damage lasts.
**Success** The target takes full damage, full persistent damage, and is dazzled while the persistent damage lasts. markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 98 **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L **Activate**Strike --- A broad wooden tube with a handle, a _sparking spellgun_ radiates warmth. You Activate the spellgun by aiming it at one creature and making your choice of a spell attack roll or a firearm attack roll against the target's AC. This spellgun has a [range increment](/Traits.aspx?ID=248) of 30 feet. The spellgun fires a small ball of sparks and fire, then crumbles to ash. The ball explodes in a flash when it hits, dealing fire damage and [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) according to its type. **Critical Success** The target takes double damage, takes double persistent damage, is [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=1) for 1 round, and is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7) while the persistent damage lasts.
**Success** The target takes full damage, full persistent damage, and is dazzled while the persistent damage lasts. Sparking Spellgun (Moderate) **Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 98 **Price** 150 gp **Bulk** L --- The damage is 7d6, and the persistent damage is 3d4. Sparking Spellgun (Greater) **Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 98 **Price** 600 gp **Bulk** L --- The damage is 10d6, and the persistent damage is 5d4.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Spiritual Warhorn](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2126)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Force" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=75" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 98

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Two Actions" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A _spiritual warhorn_ is a trumpet made of horn, leather, and metal. When you play a single, long note from the warhorn, it calls forth a number of Medium spiritual manifestations of warriors to aid you, according to the horn's type. Each warrior appears in an open square adjacent to an enemy within 60 feet of you, makes a Strike for 2d6 force damage (with an attack bonus determined by the warhorn's type), and then disappears. The warriors can flank with one another and with you and your allies.

Once the magic is used, the warhorn remains as a non-magical [musical instrument](/Equipment.aspx?ID=37).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

</column>

---

A _spiritual warhorn_ is a trumpet made of horn, leather, and metal. When you play a single, long note from the warhorn, it calls forth a number of Medium spiritual manifestations of warriors to aid you, according to the horn's type. Each warrior appears in an open square adjacent to an enemy within 60 feet of you, makes a Strike for 2d6 force damage (with an attack bonus determined by the warhorn's type), and then disappears. The warriors can flank with one another and with you and your allies.

Once the magic is used, the warhorn remains as a non-magical [musical instrument](/Equipment.aspx?ID=37).

    level="1"

>
[Talespinner's Lyre](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2127)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Auditory" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=16" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Olfactory" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=246" />
<trait label="Visual" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=163" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 98

**Price** 235 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Two Actions" /><row>([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Plucking a _talespinner's lyre_ while focusing on an event you witnessed causes the instrument to create an illusion in a 50-foot emanation that plays out your memory of the event in real time, complete with sights, sounds, and smells. You can Sustain the Activation for up to 1 minute to keep it playing. The scene reproduces only what's in its area, including nothing beyond that even if present in the memory. The scene is realistic, but all observers can clearly tell it's an illusion. Observers can't interact with the scene directly nor can they taste or touch elements of it to get a sensation you didn't personally experience, but they can attempt skill checks to discern more about the scene without altering its contents. For example, no one could see something you didn't, such as the true form of a creature polymorphed into a squirrel, but an observer might be able to use Perception and [Sense Motive](/Actions.aspx?ID=85) to discern the squirrel was acting unlike a squirrel should.

 Once the magic is used, the lyre remains as a non-magical [virtuoso instrument](/Equipment.aspx?ID=37).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Talespinner's Lyre

>
[Watchful Portrait](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2129)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Scrying" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=141" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 98

**Price** 125 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A _watchful portrait_ appears to be an innocuous depiction of a bored noble, unassuming relative, or unknown local celebrity. You Activate the portrait by hanging it. While it's activated, you can use a single action with the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) trait to see through the eyes of the portrait instead of your own. You can do so while you're within 500 feet of the portrait, even if it's outside your line of sight or line of effect. You visually observe the area around the portrait from its perspective, using your own visual senses. While you're scrying through it, the portrait's eyes follow others more than usual. A creature that succeeds at a DC 30 Perception check notices this phenomenon. The scrying ends after 10 minutes or when you decide to stop watching through the portrait.

 The painting loses its magic when the scrying ends, if 4 hours pass after you Activate it without you looking through it, or if you Activate a different _watchful portrait_. The portrait then remains a normal, non-magical work of art, but due to its rather bland subject matter, it is worth no more than 5 gp.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Watchful Portrait

When you Activate this item, you break it and choose one creature within 30 feet. The target must attempt a DC 30 Fortitude save; [amphibious](/Traits.aspx?ID=207) and [aquatic](/Traits.aspx?ID=168) creatures are immune. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The target becomes [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) and [unable to breathe](/Rules.aspx?ID=468) until this sickened condition ends.
**Failure** As success, but sickened 2.
**Critical Failure** As success, but sickened 3.
name: Whelming Scrimshaw

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 99 **Price** 50 gp **Usage** held in 2 hands **Bulk** L **Activate**([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) --- A blue finish decorates the ceramic body of a _wind ocarina_. When you play a note on this ocarina, for 1 minute, winds sweep into strong gusts in a 5-foot emanation around you. The winds have the following effects.
  • Ammunition from physical ranged attacks—such as arrows, bolts, sling bullets, and other objects of comparable size—can't pass through the area. Passing through the area causes attacks with bigger ranged weapons, such as [thrown](/Traits.aspx?ID=195) weapons, to take a –2 circumstance penalty to their attack rolls if their paths pass through the emanation. Massive ranged weapons and spell effects that don't create physical objects can pass through the emanation with no penalty.
  • The area is difficult terrain to creatures attempting to move through it.
  • Gases, including creatures in gaseous form, can't pass through the emanation. When the emanation arises, such gases are removed from the area, including your space.
  • The area, including your space, has breathable air.
Once the magic is used, the ocarina remains a non-magical [virtuoso musical instrument](/Equipment.aspx?ID=37).
name: Wind Ocarina

This large brass medallion hangs low on the torso. It’s shaped in the form of an unblinking eye, with a ring of turquoise as the iris and an orb of jet serving as the pupil. The amulet grants you a +2 item bonus to [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2298) checks. When you invest the amulet, you either increase your Wisdom modifier by 1 or increase it to +4, whichever would give you a higher value.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You cast [_truesight_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727).

</column>

</title>

<traits>

</row>

</column>

---

Each of these shining, bluish-gray metal armbands is adorned with the plated visage of a [gorgon's](/Monsters.aspx?ID=686) head. When targeted by a spell or effect with the [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93) effect, you treat the result of your save as if it were one degree of success better, and the result of any check made to inflict such an effect on you as one degree of success worse (as if you were more than twice the rank of the spell or effect targeting you). When you invest the armbands, you either increase your Constitution modifier by 1 or increase it to +4, whichever would give you a higher value.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You clap the bracers together and remove a single condition of your choice currently afflicting you. If the condition is permanent, it's instead suppressed for 1 hour.

</column>

These seemingly ordinary rectangular eyeglasses feature clear lenses framed in copper. When invested and worn, they rest perfectly on the bridge of the nose and can only be removed by the wearer. You gain a +3 item bonus to [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) checks and any skill check made to [Identify Magic](/Actions.aspx?ID=24). When you invest the spectacles, you either increase your Intelligence modifier by 1 or increase it to +4, whichever would give you a higher value. You must select the skills and languages the first time you invest the item, and whenever you invest the same _artificer spectacles_, you get the same skills and languages you chose the first time.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You cast a 3rd-rank [_mending_](/Spells.aspx?ID=189) spell on an item you touch.

---

While the insides of these boots are comfortable, fur-lined leather, the outsides are a jumble of slate plates, giving the impression of a rockslide. You gain a +3 item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) checks and a +2 circumstance bonus to [Force Open](/Actions.aspx?ID=34) and [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=38). When you invest the boots, you either increase your Strength modifier by 1 or increase it to +4, whichever would give you a higher value.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** You succeed or critically succeed with a Shove;  **Effect** If the Shove was a success, you push your opponent up to 10 feet instead of 5 feet. If the Shove was a critical success, you push your opponent up to 20 feet, and you can then choose to knock them [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31).

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 20"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Beguiling Crown](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2136)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Apex" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=10" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 103

**Price** 80,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn headwear

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This hugely massive crown is bedazzled with glimmering jewels and enchanted with powerful magics that make the gold seem to ripple and surge like strange glimmering tide pools. While uncomfortable to wear, the crown beguiles those within the wearer's presence. Creatures within 30 feet of you automatically improve their attitude toward you by one step (up to [friendly](/Conditions.aspx?ID=18)). This doesn't prevent [hostile](/Conditions.aspx?ID=23) creatures from attacking you, but it might give you a chance to talk to them before they strike. The dazzling nature of the crown makes it hard for you to read the intentions of others, and you take a –4 status penalty to your Perception DC when someone uses [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) against you. When you invest the crown, you either increase your Charisma modifier by 1 or increase it to +4, whichever would give you a higher value.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per round;   **Effect** You're the subject of the [_sanctuary_](/Spells.aspx?ID=266) spell until the end of your next turn.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** A creature succeeds at an attack roll against you;  **Effect** The creature must succeed at a DC 41 Will save or the attack roll becomes a failure and the target is friendly to you until the end of its turn. On a critical failure, the target becomes friendly to you, drops to their knees, and begs your forgiveness for 1 minute or until another creature takes a [hostile action](/Rules.aspx?ID=300) against them.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** Choose one living creature within 30 feet of you. That creature must succeed at a DC 41 Will saving throw or become [helpful](/Conditions.aspx?ID=21) to you for the next 24 hours. If they succeed, they become friendly to you for 1 hour. If they critically succeed they're immune to this effect for 1 year.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Beguiling Crown

Constructed of dull gray metal, this [breastplate](/Armor.aspx?ID=10) is decorated with the symbol of a craggy black mountaintop. This breastplate functions as a _+3 [greater resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=279) breastplate_. When you're [Shoved](/Actions.aspx?ID=38) or otherwise [forced to move](/Rules.aspx?ID=451), you can reduce the amount you move by up to 10 feet. When you invest the breastplate, you either increase your Constitution modifier by 1 or increase it to +4, whichever would give you a higher value.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You pound a solid and constant rhythm on your breastplate and cast a 7th-level [_regeneration_](/Spells.aspx?ID=248) spell on yourself.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** With a single tap, you gain immunity to [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126) for 1 minute.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You fail or critically fail a Fortitude saving throw;  **Effect** If you failed the saving throw, it becomes a success. If you critically failed, it becomes a failure instead.

</column>

<traits>

<trait label="Fortune" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=76" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 103

**Price** 70,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn circlet

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This silvery, woven ring of leaves sits on top of the head. While wearing it, when you roll Perception for initiative, you can roll twice and take the higher result. This is a fortune effect. Whenever you spend at least 1 minute talking with a living creature, you automatically become aware of its attitude toward you. When you invest the laurel, you either increase your Wisdom modifier by 1 or increase it to +4, whichever would give you a higher value.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per minute; **Trigger** You're hit by an attack;  **Effect** You take half damage from the triggering attack.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** You fail, but don't critically fail, a saving throw;  **Effect** You anticipate the danger and guard against it, often in unconventional or almost inconceivable ways. Treat the failed saving throw as a success.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> 10 minutes ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You spend 10 minutes talking with one living creature, conversing in inspirational, religious, or philosophical terms. You gain valuable insights into the personality of your target—their hopes, dreams, and fears. When the ritual is over, you gain a +4 item bonus to all Perception checks made concerning the target for one month. Also, the target gains inspirational insight, allowing the target to use one of the two reactions listed above once during the next 24 hours.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Laurel of the Empath

This ragged cloak doesn't appear to be anything special at first glance, seemingly made from mangy black bear fur with various rings of blackened iron piercing the edge of the skin. While somewhat off-putting, the mantle grants a +2 status bonus to all Fortitude saving throws against [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46) and [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126). When you invest the mantle, you either increase your Constitution modifier by 1 or increase it to +4, whichever is higher.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per round; **Trigger** You take damage;  **Effect** Drawing the cloak around you, you reduce the damage taken by 10.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** If you're currently afflicted by a poison or a disease, you can hold the cloak tight to your body and immediately attempt a saving throw to end the effect. If that saving throw succeeds, you end the effect of either the poison or disease no matter the stage of the affliction. Furthermore, you gain immunity to that poison or disease for 24 hours.

>
[Sandals of the Stag](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2142)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Apex" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=10" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 104

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn shoes

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These sturdy leather sandals feature straps that wrap up to the knees. Etched in the leather are intricate patterns of stags leaping through the forest. You gain a +5-foot item bonus to your land Speed and a +3 item bonus on [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) checks when attempting to [High Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=2377) or [Long Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=2378). When you invest the sandals, you either increase your Strength modifier by 1 or increase it to +4, whichever would give you a higher value.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per minute; **Trigger** You attempt a High Jump or Long Jump but you didn't Stride at least 10 feet;  **Effect** You can attempt the jump normally. It doesn't automatically fail.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Sandals of the Stag

[Shadowmist Cape](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2143)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Apex" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=10" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 104

**Price** 40,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn cloak

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This black cape flows more like a vaporous liquid than fabric. The cape grants you a +3 item bonus to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) checks. When you invest the cape, you either increase your Dexterity modifier by 1 or increase it to +4, whichever would give you a higher value
.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** A creature misses you with an attack;  **Effect** Attempt a Stealth check against the triggering creature's Perception DC. If you roll a success, you're [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=22) from that creature until the end of your next turn or until right after you use a [hostile action](/Rules.aspx?ID=300) against that creature. If you roll a critical success, you're hidden from that creature until the end of your next turn, even if you use hostile actions against that creature.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** With a twirl of the cape, you transform yourself into a puff of gray smoke. You cast [_vapor form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1738) on yourself.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Shadowmist Cape

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 20"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Viper Rapier](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2145)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Apex" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=10" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 104

**Price** 70,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Rapier](/Weapons.aspx?ID=36)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Crafted from flawless jade, the guard of this [rapier](/Weapons.aspx?ID=36) takes the form of a twisting serpent with the blade thrusting from its open mouth. This rapier functions as a _+3 [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [quickstrike](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2844) rapier_. While wielding the rapier, your movement doesn't trigger reactions when you Stride, [Balance](/Actions.aspx?ID=28), or [Tumble Through](/Actions.aspx?ID=29). When you invest the rapier, you either increase your Dexterity modifier by 1 or increase it to +4, whichever would give you a higher value.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You fail, but don't critically fail, an attack using the _viper rapier_;  **Effect** The tip of the rapier briefly takes the form of a viper's head, and the blade twists and contorts, biting the target you missed. The target is affected as if successfully poisoned with [cave worm venom](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3328) (DC 43).

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561))  **Trigger** A creature misses or critically misses you with an attack;  **Effect** With a slight, obviously mocking bow or curtsy, you Step.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You twirl your rapier in a serpentine pattern, causing your form to become a blur of motion. You're [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) for 1 minute or until you take a [hostile action](/Rules.aspx?ID=300). While you're concealed, you also gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Reflex saving throws.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Viper Rapier

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 20"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Whispering Staff](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2146)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Apex" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=10" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Staff" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=151" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 105

**Price** 70,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This gnarled wooden staff is carved with humanoid faces in various emotional states. When the staff is activated, the faces begin to whisper a variety of languages in sibilant tones, creating what seems to be nonsense to all but the staff's wielder or those they choose to affect. The staff functions as a [_major staff of the unblinking eye_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3048). While using the staff, you gain a +3 item bonus to [Decipher Writing](/Actions.aspx?ID=22), [Identify Magic](/Actions.aspx?ID=24), and [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26) checks, regardless of the skill. When you invest the staff, you either increase your Intelligence modifier by 1 or increase it to +4, whichever would give you a higher value. You must select the skills and languages the first time you invest the item, and whenever you invest the same _whispering staff_, you get the same skills and languages you chose the first time.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Frequency** once per round;   **Effect** Through the staff's strange whispering, you gain a glimpse into the mind and desires of one creature you can see within 30 feet. Until the end of your next turn, that creature is [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) to you and takes a –2 circumstance penalty to saving throws against your spells.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [misfortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=654)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You point the staff at one creature you can see within 30 feet of you, causing the whispers to howl in that creature's mind. The target must attempt a DC 43 Will save. If it fails, whenever the creature attempts an attack roll, skill check, or saving throw, it must roll twice and take the worse roll. This lasts until the start of your next turn.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You twirl the staff in three consecutive circles and call for the whispers to speak up. For the next minute, you and all allies within a 30-foot emanation around you can hear your staff's whispers clearly and distinctly, gaining benefit from their advice and mental protection. Whenever you and your affected allies attempt to Recall Knowledge or attempt a saving throw against a [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) effect, you roll twice and take the better result. This is a [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=76) effect.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Whispering Staff

This cluster of sensitive wire whiskers fits over the nose or snout to provide sensory information as it reacts to nearby odors and other scents. A creature wearing _olfactory stimulators_ gains a sense of smell, which is as precise as an average member of its species, as well as the [scent](/Rules.aspx?ID=417) special ability if members of its species typically have that ability. _Olfactory stimulators_ can be fitted to animal companions as well as sapient creatures; stimulators produced for companion use have the [companion](/Traits.aspx?ID=29) trait.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You attempt a saving throw against an [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60) effect; **Requirements** Your animal companion wearing the _empathy charm_ is within 10 feet; **Effect** Your animal companion senses the effect and attempts to calm you. ---

The city of Riddleport is notorious for its noxious air and water. While some inhabitants tolerate the haze, most wear bootstrap respirators to filter out as many of the pollutants as possible. Designed by dwarf smiths operating the Gas Forges, the flexible face mask of a bootstrap respirator fits snugly over your mouth and nose, fastened by two adjustable leather straps. A manual pump inserted into your footwear or under the arm circulates air through tubes and over filters fitted into the front of the mask. While wearing a bootstrap respirator, you gain a +1 item bonus to any saves that require you to smell or taste, such as [inhaled](/Traits.aspx?ID=96) [poisons](/Traits.aspx?ID=126) and airborne [diseases](/Traits.aspx?ID=46).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Bootstrap Respirator

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Sonic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=147" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Bulk** L

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A specialized _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [thundering](/Equipment.aspx?ID=306) [probing cane](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1346)_ made from strengthened spruce wood, a _batsbreath cane_ is distinctive for its brass tip. The tip covers a small hollow in the wood that houses quartz crystals infused with latent storm magic.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You strike the cane firmly against the ground, causing a pin within the brass tip to tap the crystals and emit a sonic pulse. The pulse reverberates in a 60-foot radius for the next minute, with the cane acting as an antenna to receive the echoes. For 1 minute, as long as you remain in the area and are holding the cane, you gain hearing as a [precise sense](/Rules.aspx?ID=411).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Batsbreath Cane

This prosthetic eye converts visible light into a telepathic signal that is relayed to the wearer's mind using divination magic. As the wearer's mind must process the telepathic signal in the same way as it would a nerve impulse, the acuity and other abilities related to the vision provided by the _magical prosthetic eye_ matches that of other members of your ancestry (for instance, a [goblin](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=4) with a magical prosthetic eye would be able to see in [darkvision](/Rules.aspx?ID=415), while a [human](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=6) wearing the same prosthetic would need illumination). You can remove or replace a _magical prosthetic eye_ using an Interact action.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Magical Prosthetic Eye

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You become keenly aware of your foes, even those seemingly out of reach. For 1 minute, you can close your eyes as a free action to see through a ranged weapon you're wielding, which reduces the penalty for firing into your weapon's second [range increment](/Traits.aspx?ID=248) from –2 to 0. This effect doesn't negate the [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=1) condition.

These discrete prosthetics have been the secret behind more than a few salacious rumors getting out among Taldan high society. This functions like a [_magical prosthetic eye_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2156), but with an added benefit.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You whisper “spy for me” to the eye, which removes itself from your eye socket and begins to relay its signal to you even at range. Although it can't move on its own, you can place the eye in a discrete location (using your [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) DC) to avoid detection. For 10 minutes, you can see what the eye sees as long as you're within 100 feet of the eye. The eye's signal can penetrate most barriers but is blocked by lead of any thickness, as well as denser materials. The eye's signal is visual only.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561))    **Effect** You cause the arm to become corporeal until the end of your turn, allowing you to use it to make Strikes or grasp objects. It remains corporeal if you’re holding an item or it’s wearing an item at the end of your turn. Your unarmed attack Strikes made with the aether appendage are [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644). If they’re already magical, they instead gain the effect of the [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2840) property rune.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;  **Requirements** You're holding a non-magical item of light or negligible Bulk; **Effect** The item becomes incorporeal for 1 minute. Your _aether appendage_ can use the incorporeal item normally.

---

Built with collapsible poles and expanding hoops, this tail can extend to a length of 20 feet. While it's no more prehensile than a tail of a typical member of your ancestry, it ends with an anchor that you can secure around a sturdy object with an Interact action. While the tail is anchored, you can't move more than 20 feet from that spot, but you can use the tail to lower yourself up to 20 feet, as if it were a length of [rope](/Equipment.aspx?ID=44). You can use another Interact action to disengage the anchor and retract your tail.

Kobolds admire well-designed objects, especially if it gives them an opportunity to pack it with traps and surprises. This prosthetic tail hides numerous blades and spikes, tensioned and wound around a spring-loaded trigger at the base of the prosthesis. Resetting and reattaching a sprung thrasher tail takes 10 minutes.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> ;   **Trigger** You're [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20);  **Effect** Your tail comes off in your opponent's hand, and the mechanism unwinds, causing the blades and spikes to protrude and the tail to spin and thrash. The tail deals 8d6 slashing damage to the opponent who has you grabbed with a DC 25 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. Regardless of the result of their save, you're no longer grabbed.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

This [wheelchair](/Rules.aspx?ID=1634) is outfitted with a variety of tools and devices to assist with the creation and production of a number of mechanical implements. While seated in a _chair of inventions_, you have a worn [superb repair kit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=43) that doesn't count against your Bulk limit or maximum worn items.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** The chair deploys a complete [expanded alchemist's lab](/Equipment.aspx?ID=543). The chair is immobile while this lab is deployed, but levers and gears in the chair allow you to easily retrieve and access everything you need from both the attached superb repair kit and the deployed lab to [Craft](/Actions.aspx?ID=43). This setup is highly efficient and gives you a +2 circumstance bonus to [Earn Income](/Actions.aspx?ID=23) using [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4).

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Amazing Pop-Up Book](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2170)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Grimoire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=387" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 110

**Price** 1,250 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

[Goblin](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=4) [wizards](/Classes.aspx?ID=12) invented the amazing pop-up book to store their spells without written words, though the tradition has been spreading to illusionists from other cultures. These grimoires have colorful covers and open to reveal three-dimensional scenes illustrating various spells. Goblins delight in constructing the books just right so a terrifying creature, like a horse or dog, pops up toward the reader each time a page is turned.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [spellshape](/Traits.aspx?ID=513)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** If your next action is to cast an [illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=629) spell prepared from this grimoire that creates illusory terrain or creatures, such as [_mirage_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1604), you draw forth the pop-up scene. If a creature succeeds, but doesn’t critically succeed, at a saving throw to [disbelieve](/Rules.aspx?ID=267) the illusion, then just as their mind recognizes the illusion, a startling—though obviously false—illusion pops up somewhere unexpected in their field of vision, visible to only them, dealing mental damage equal to the spell’s rank.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Amazing Pop-Up Book

    right="Item 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Architect's Pattern Book](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2171)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Grimoire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=387" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 110

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Items which can be used once per week, may be used once per scenario or adventure in Pathfinder Society play._

**Price** 240 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Typically created by [wizards](/Classes.aspx?ID=12) who are hobbyists or professional architects, an _architect's pattern book_ allows the caster  to customize certain spells that create magical structures or domiciles, adding recreational areas such as an indoor bathhouse, gaming room, swimming pool, or similar luxury.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [spellshape](/Traits.aspx?ID=513)) **Frequency** once per week;   **Effect** If your next action is to cast a [_cozy cabin_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1474), [_planar palace_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1634), or [_resplendent mansion_](/Spells.aspx?ID=2018) spell, you add a room to the structure that is up to 10 feet per side per rank of the spell. This room is outfitted with all the accoutrements for a particular type of recreation, determined by you when you cast the spell. Any character who spends at least 1 hour using this recreational facility and then sleeps a full 8 hours inside the location created by the spell is exceptionally well-rested. They regain double the amount of Hit Points they would normally receive for an 8-hour rest, and when they make the next day’s preparations, they gain a +1 circumstance bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) checks and Will saves for the next 12 hours.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Architect's Pattern Book

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** Your last action was to cast a prepared divine spell granted by your deity;  **Effect** You and all your allies in a 30-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) gain [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) 10 to [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737) damage for 1 minute. You can instead choose to grant resistance to holy effects instead of spirit damage if the spell you cast had the [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) trait, or resistance to [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521) effects instead of spirit if your spell had the unholy trait.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 20"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Codex of Destruction and Renewal](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2173)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Grimoire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=387" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 111

**Price** 70,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

An unsmiling mask, half obsidian and half ivory, is embossed on the cover of this weighty tome, the opposite color forming the background of each half. Each _codex of destruction and renewal_ is said to contain a fragment of the knowledge [Nethys](/Deities.aspx?ID=12) gained in his apotheosis and they're sacred to his church.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [spellshape](/Traits.aspx?ID=513)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** If your next action is to cast a damage-dealing spell prepared from this grimoire, the spell wreaks even greater destruction. The spell ignores 10 [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) and you reroll any damage dice that roll a 1. If the spell reduces a creature to 0 Hit Points, they’re torn apart at an infinitesimal level, not even leaving a fine ash; this is a [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571) effect.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [spellshape](/Traits.aspx?ID=513)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** If your next action is to cast a [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623) spell prepared from this grimoire that restores Hit Points, the spell renews the target constantly and perfectly. The spell grants the target [regeneration](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=72) 20, restoring 20 Hit Points at the start of its turn and preventing the target both from [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=69) due to damage and from its dying condition increasing to a value that would result in its death. Each time the creature regains Hit Points from regeneration, it regrows all damaged or ruined organs; it can also regrow any severed body parts as a free action immediately after the body part is severed, with the original crumbling to ash. The effect lasts for 4 rounds.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Codex of Destruction and Renewal

<traits>
<trait label="Grimoire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=387" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 111

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L
</row>

</row>

---

This pageless grimoire is made up of two durable covers that open to display a small ritual circle. When you first invest the grimoire, you and your familiar each press a limb to a corner of the mat. The ritual circle then morphs to one matching the tradition of your patron, the spells contained in your familiar appearing in the margins. During your daily preparations, your familiar performs a small jaunt around the open ritual circle to strengthen its connection to your patron.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [spellshape](/Traits.aspx?ID=513)) **Frequency** once per 10 minutes;   **Effect** If your next action is to cast a spell your familiar learned from a lesson (whether a cantrip or from a spell slot), your patron notices that you’re putting their power to good use and strengthens your familiar with a surge of magic. Your familiar [Sustains](/Actions.aspx?ID=2631) one of your spells.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Communion Mat

    level="1"
    right="Item 5"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Corrosive Engravings](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2175)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Acid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=3" />
<trait label="Grimoire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=387" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 111

**Price** 140 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These tin sheets are bound in brass and show significant signs of erosion. The grimoire's title is acid-etched, and flipping between the sheets leaves your fingers covered in flecks of rust and powdery metal.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** If your next action is to cast an [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=3) or [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126) spell that deals [persistent damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29), any creature who takes persistent damage from the spell is also [sickened 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) until the persistent damage ends. Using an action to retch can reduce the sickened value as normal, but it can't reduce the sickened value below 1 until the persistent damage ends.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Corrosive Engravings

>
[Courtier's Pillow Book](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2176)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Grimoire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=387" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 112

**Price** 250 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This elegant journal has a detailed social dossier on the owner's acquaintances. When you make your daily preparations, you can inscribe a secret or embarrassing foible about a specific individual that you know to be true or have on good authority.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** If your next action is to cast a [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) spell on a target about whom you’ve written an entry in the book, you can state that secret or foible before [Casting the Spell](/Rules.aspx?ID=3144) to give the target a –1 circumstance penalty to their saving throw against the spell. The inscription then disappears from the grimoire.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Courtier's Pillow Book

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Draxie's Recipe Book](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2177)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Grimoire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=387" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 112

**Price** 90 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This tiny recipe book was created by a famous [draxie](/Heritages.aspx?ID=140) chef, but it instantly resizes to fit the hand and eyes of the reader. While most of the pages are blank and ready to receive spells, the first four pages are taken up by a complex seasoning recipe that requires a casting of [_revealing light_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1653).

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** If your next action is to cast a _revealing light_ spell, all creatures within the spell’s area who don’t critically succeed at their save are covered with a spicy red powder. Any attempt to [grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) or grapple a creature affected in this way, or to [swallow](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=77) such a creature whole, gains a +1 circumstance bonus to the attempt, or a +2 circumstance bonus if the attempt is made using a [jaws](/Weapons.aspx?ID=356) or similar mouth-based attack, due to the target’s extra deliciousness and savory smell. An affected creature can remove the powder by thoroughly cleaning themselves (a process that typically takes about 10 minutes) or by completely immersing themselves in water. This ability can also be used to properly season up to 100 pounds of prepared food within the area of the _revealing light_ spell instantaneously.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Draxie's Recipe Book

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Harrow Spellcards](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2178)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Grimoire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=387" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 112

**Price** 425 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Crafted of sturdy paper, each card of this [harrow deck](/Equipment.aspx?ID=824) showcases a beautiful watercolor illustration with space to inscribe a spell below. When shuffled, its cards seem to fly between one another of their own accord.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** Your last action was to cast a spell prepared from this grimoire that has the [detection](/Traits.aspx?ID=574), [prediction](/Traits.aspx?ID=674), [revelation](/Traits.aspx?ID=686), or [scrying](/Traits.aspx?ID=689) trait;  **Effect** You draw forth a card to gain insight into future challenges you’ll face. Draw a card from a harrow deck or roll 1d6: 1 = hammers ([Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36)), 2 = keys ([Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34)), 3 = shields ([Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49)), 4 = books (any [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2367)), 5 = stars ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)), 6 = crowns ([Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39)). The next time you attempt a check of the same type as your result, roll twice and take the better result, as the spirits of the harrow guide your actions. If not used by your next [daily preparations](/Rules.aspx?ID=2575), this benefit disappears.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Harrow Spellcards

[Illuminated Folio](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2179)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Grimoire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=387" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />

<column gap="tiny">

**Price** 4,200 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The pages of this part-spellbook, part-bestiary are illustrated with all manner of creatures, magical and mundane. The illustrations shift and move around the page when examined.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [spellshape](/Traits.aspx?ID=513))    **Effect** If your next action is to cast a [summon](/Traits.aspx?ID=520) spell prepared from this grimoire, you summon creatures from the illuminated folio rather than their usual source. These summoned creatures appear as living illustrations, granting them [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) to physical damage equal to half their level and weakness 5 to fire and to any ability with the water trait. They can also fold themselves up to pass through spaces only an inch or so wide as part of their movement.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Illuminated Folio

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 112

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Items with effects that have an undetermined duration, end their effects at the end of a scenario or adventure unless specifically stated otherwise by the campaign rules._

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This thick book has several colorful bookmarks and page dividers. Its title and text shift between numerous languages.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;  **Requirements** Your last action was to cast a spell prepared from this grimoire that allows understanding of another language, such as [_translate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1723) or [_truespeech_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728); **Effect** The grimoire absorbs knowledge of one language translated this way (caster’s choice if more than one), allowing its bearer to communicate on a rudimentary level in that language even after the spell’s duration has elapsed. The _linguist’s dictionary_ can hold one language at a time.

markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 112 **Price** 21,000 gp **Bulk** L --- Shadows swirl around this soot-black tome, swallowing up any light that touches them. A faint whispering emanates from the grimoire's pages when opened. **Activate** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day; **Requirements** Your last action was to cast a spell prepared from this grimoire that has the [shadow](/Traits.aspx?ID=143) trait; **Effect** Your shadow, and that of the tome, elongates and reaches hungrily for one foe within 30 feet, who must attempt a Fortitude save. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=1) for 1 round and [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10) as the shadows scrape across it.
**Failure** The creature is blinded for 1 minute and drained 2 as the shadows seize it.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the shadows also pull the creature into the tome, teleporting it to the [Netherworld](/Planes.aspx?ID=36).
name: Maw of Hungry Shadows
